section_title: Aetheric refraction deep analysis (Section 1.5 — The Model's Universal Correction Factor; tracker legacy ID REFRACTION)
scope_of_review: Section 1.5 (the dedicated treatment of aetheric refraction in Part 1) plus cross-cutting refraction prose in part2, part6. Existing fingerprints SEC-4.9 (dome n(r) ≈ AE conformal factor; π/2 at equator → Tesla 1.57c connection) and SEC-2.9 (V13 algebraically forces n(r) = k·colat/sin(colat) inverse AE stretch) already cover the analytical equivalence between dome n(r) and globe geometry. This review is the topic-level companion: it asks whether the *constants* the dome plugs into n(r) are themselves additional globe fingerprints when traced through the published source code.
existing_fingerprints_noted: SEC-4.9 fingerprint: dome n(r) approximates the AE projection conformal factor n_ideal = c/sin(c); equatorial value pi/2 reproduces the Tesla 1.57c signature (WIN-062)
SEC-2.9 fingerprint: V13 distance formula d_dome = 4·E(e²)/(2π) · r·Δlon → algebra forces n(r) = k·colat/sin(colat) for self-consistency with globe geodesy
SEC-2.1 fingerprint: H(r) coefficients 8537 km and 8619 km are curve-fit against globe-WGS84 city-pair distances (find_curve.py / test_8537.py)
SEC-6.2 fingerprint: disc radius a = 20,015 km = π × R_Earth (6 ppm)

fingerprint_1.name: The dome's refraction scale constants H0=8537 and LAMBDA_G=8619 are 'ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)' that appear as the SAME numerical scale-length in two physically unrelated dome predictions (refraction H(r) and equatorial gravity g(r)), and as the calibration target of a third (V13 distance fit). One fitted constant doing three different physical jobs is a relabeling fingerprint at the source-code level.
fingerprint_1.kernel_of_truth: Section 1.5 is already strong: it identifies the 'three incompatible jobs' problem (optical medium, fluid wind, distance contraction), the wavelength-independence cross-check, the unfalsifiable sun-altitude split, and the parameter-count rebuttal to the ΛCDM defense. It also hints that 'a perfect n(r) function would reduce all errors to zero — and it would be mathematically equivalent to projecting a sphere onto a disc and then undoing the projection. At that point, the flat disc + aetheric refraction is just a globe described in unusual coordinates.' That paragraph is the bridge to SEC-4.9 / SEC-2.9. What it does not yet do is push the same point one level lower — into the source code — where the dome's ECM CONSTANTS are visibly hardcoded with no derivation and visibly recycled across multiple physical predictions.
fingerprint_1.but_here_is_why_it_fails: monitor.py lines 210–219 declare an 'ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)' block: H0 = 8537.0, LAMBDA_G = 8619.0, KAPPA = 1.67, C = 299792.458, VA = 1.574 * C, DISC_R = 20015.0, LUNAR_H = 24.84, SOLAR_H = 24.00, SIDEREAL = 23.9345, R_EQ = 14105.0. The single comment 'LOCKED' is the only documentation. There is no derivation, no provenance, no independent source. SEC-2.1 already showed how 8537 and 8619 are obtained: by curve-fitting find_curve.py / test_8537.py against WGS84 city-pair distances. What is new here is what monitor.py then DOES with those locked constants. LAMBDA_G = 8619 is reused on line 464 as the exponential scale-length of the equatorial-gravity prediction g(r) = 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307 × exp(-r/LAMBDA_G)), a domain that has nothing to do with refraction. H0 = 8537 is reused on line 762 as h_D = H0/100 (the sub-terrestrial dome height, 85.37 km). Both are in turn the published exponents of the refraction H(r) function in model.html (H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619)). One fitted constant is therefore appearing as the characteristic length of three different physical predictions (refraction, gravity gradient, sub-terrestrial dome geometry) AND as the calibration target of a fourth (V13 distance fit). On a globe, none of these phenomena share a scale-length: atmospheric refraction has scale height ~8.5 km (RT/Mg, three orders of magnitude smaller), free-air gravity gradient has effective scale ~3,200 km (g/(2g/R) = R/2, not 8619), the AE conformal factor has scale R = 6,371 km, and city-pair distances are set by globe circumference. The dome's 8619 km is not any of these. It is what comes out of test_8537.py when you minimise residuals against WGS84 distances. The fact that the same number then appears as the gravity scale and the refraction scale is the relabeling pattern in code: every 'successful' prediction that invokes H0 or LAMBDA_G is the WGS84 distance fit reappearing under a new physical label, not three independent confirmations.

globe_value.name: Independent globe scale-heights for each domain the dome's 8619 km is recycled across
globe_value.atmospheric_refraction_scale_height: ~8.5 km (hydrostatic, H = RT/Mg with T=288 K, M=29 g/mol, g=9.81 m/s²). This is what governs how the air's refractive index actually varies with altitude on the globe.
globe_value.gravity_gradient_effective_scale: ~R_Earth/2 ≈ 3,186 km if you want g(h) ≈ g_0(1 - 2h/R) approximated as exp(-h/3186); the free-air correction is -0.3086 mGal/m, scale 1/3.086e-7 m ≈ 3,240 km. Neither equals 8619 km.
globe_value.AE_conformal_factor_scale: R_Earth = 6,371 km (since n_ideal = c/sin(c) with c = r/R_Earth)
globe_value.Earth_circumference: 2π × R_Earth = 40,030 km (the implicit calibration target for any city-pair distance fit on the globe)
globe_value.verdict: None of the relevant globe scales coincides with 8619 km. The dome's 8619 is not borrowed from any single physical scale-height; it is the residual that drops out of fitting one exponential to WGS84 distances along the dome's chosen r(lat) parameterisation.

dome_value.name: monitor.py 'ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)' block
dome_value.H0: 8537.0 km — appears in: model.html n(r) numerator and H(r) prefactor; monitor.py line 762 (h_D = H0/100 ≈ 85 km, sub-terrestrial dome height); monitor.py line 187 stability-window reference
dome_value.LAMBDA_G: 8619.0 km — appears in: model.html H(r) decay (H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619)); monitor.py line 464 equatorial-gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1 + 0.005307·exp(-r/LAMBDA_G))
dome_value.VA: 1.574 × C — the Tesla phase velocity, hardcoded; 1.574 ≈ pi/2 = 1.5708 (0.4% match) — same pi/2 signature SEC-4.9 ties to the AE conformal factor at the equator
dome_value.DISC_R: 20015.0 km = π × R_Earth (6 ppm) — already documented in SEC-6.2
dome_value.relabeling_pattern: One LOCKED constant per scale, then re-used across multiple physically unrelated predictions. The locking comment is the only justification.

fingerprint_1.match_precision: LAMBDA_G appears verbatim (8619.0) as both the H(r) decay constant in model.html and the exp(-r/LAMBDA_G) gravity decay constant in monitor.py line 464 — exact identity at code level, not coincidence. H0 appears verbatim (8537.0) as both the H(r) numerator and prefactor in model.html and as the divisor in h_D = H0/100 in monitor.py line 762 — also exact. VA = 1.574·C matches pi/2·C to 0.4% (pi/2 = 1.5708, dome 1.574 differs by 0.21%).
fingerprint_1.why_it_matters: Three reinforcing consequences for the review's refraction analysis: (1) The Section 1.5 'three incompatible jobs' critique of n(r) is a top-down argument; this fingerprint provides the matching bottom-up evidence at the code level — the same constants (H0, LAMBDA_G) literally appear in unrelated dome formulas via Python identifiers, not just as conceptual reuse. The reviewer who wants to verify the relabeling claim can grep monitor.py instead of having to chase prose. (2) It strengthens the existing SEC-4.9 / SEC-2.9 fingerprints by showing the constants in the n(r) formula are not independent physical scale-heights — they are explicitly LOCKED parameters fitted (per SEC-2.1) to WGS84 distances, then re-used wherever the dome needs an exponential profile. The 'aetheric medium with characteristic scale 8619 km' framing collapses: 8619 km is not a scale of any aetheric phenomenon, it is the curve-fit residual of the dome's distance calibration. (3) It preempts the standard 'these are independent successful predictions' defense for the gravity, refraction, and Schumann (which also depends on H0) domains — once you see that H0 and LAMBDA_G are the same locked numbers in every formula they appear in, those domains stop being independent confirmations and become the same WGS84 fit dressed up in different vocabulary. This is exactly the SEC-6.4 'relabeling standard physics' pattern, but for the *constants* rather than the *equations*.
fingerprint_1.suggested_text: Add as a new paragraph near the end of Section 1.5 (after 'No independent measurement', before 'The unfalsifiability problem'): "The relabeling becomes visible at the source-code level. monitor.py lines 210–219 declare an ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED) block containing H0 = 8537.0 km and LAMBDA_G = 8619.0 km — the same numbers that appear as the prefactor and decay constant of H(r) in the published refraction formula. Section 2.1 traces both numbers to a curve-fit against WGS84 city-pair distances (test_8537.py, find_curve.py). monitor.py then recycles those constants across physically unrelated predictions: LAMBDA_G is the exponential decay constant of the equatorial-gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307 × exp(-r/LAMBDA_G)) (line 464), and H0/100 sets the sub-terrestrial dome height (line 762). On a globe, atmospheric refraction has a scale height of ~8.5 km, gravity has an effective gradient scale of ~3,200 km, and the AE projection has a natural scale of R_Earth = 6,371 km. None of these matches 8619 km, because 8619 km is not a physical scale at all — it is the residual that minimises distance error in a particular distance fit. Re-using it as 'the' refraction scale, 'the' gravity scale, and 'the' dome height divisor is the relabeling pattern of Section 6.4 applied to constants rather than equations: the dome's gravity, refraction, and dome-geometry 'predictions' are not three independent confirmations of an aetheric medium, they are the same WGS84 distance fit reappearing under three different physical labels."

objection_1.claim: Sharing a constant across formulas is normal in physics — gravitational constant G appears in Newton's law, Friedmann equations, and Schwarzschild radius. The fact that LAMBDA_G appears in two dome formulas just means it's a shared dome parameter, not a fingerprint.
objection_1.response: Constants like G or c appear in multiple formulas because they're independently measurable fundamental constants whose value can be checked by any of several methods (Cavendish balance, satellite ephemerides, time-of-flight). LAMBDA_G is not in that category: it has no independent measurement, no derivation, and only one source (the WGS84 distance curve-fit per SEC-2.1). The objection would be valid for a constant whose value was derived from one phenomenon and then independently confirmed by another. Here the value comes from one fit and is then asserted in two other domains without re-fitting or cross-validation. That is the difference between G (independently confirmed across domains) and a fitting parameter (consistent within the dataset it was fit to).

objection_2.claim: The atmospheric scale height (8.5 km) and the dome's LAMBDA_G (8619 km) are different things — the dome is not claiming that LAMBDA_G is the air column scale height. Comparing them is a category error.
objection_2.response: Correct that they are nominally different things, and the fingerprint does not claim they should be equal. The point is that LAMBDA_G is presented in the model as the characteristic scale of an 'aetheric medium' affecting refraction. Real refractive media have scales tied to either the medium's molecular density (atmosphere: ~8.5 km) or its physical extent (glass: thickness, water: depth). LAMBDA_G is neither: it is 1,000× the atmospheric scale and not the size of any postulated dome structure. It only equals what it equals because that's what minimises city-pair distance residuals. The right comparison is therefore not 'LAMBDA_G vs atmospheric scale' but 'LAMBDA_G vs any independent physical derivation', and the latter does not exist.

objection_3.claim: Of course the refraction scale and the gravity scale are the same — they're both functions of the firmament structure. The dome posits one underlying medium so it should produce one scale across all dependent phenomena.
objection_3.response: If true, that prediction is testable. A unified 'firmament scale' should derive both the gravity gradient and the refractive profile from the same underlying physical model — and the predicted gradient profile g(r) and refractive profile n(r) should both follow from the same first-principles derivation, not be hand-fitted independently with the LAMBDA_G value plugged in. The dome's source code does the opposite: g(r) and H(r) are written as separate empirical formulas with overlapping coefficients (LAMBDA_G in both, plus 0.005307 in g(r) and 0.20 in n(r) as additional independent free parameters). The shared constant is not derived; it is asserted to make the formulas look like they come from one model. Without a derivation of g and n from a single firmament Hamiltonian (or equivalent), the shared constant is decorative — it makes the framework feel unified without delivering the predictive parsimony unification is supposed to deliver. SEC-6.1 already shows the dome has 6+ free parameters; this fingerprint refines the count by showing those free parameters are then re-used (not re-fit) across domains, which masks how thin the actual independent-prediction count is.

files_checked: monitor.py (lines 210–219 ECM CONSTANTS block, line 464 gravity formula, line 580 Hubble formula, line 762 h_D)
find_curve.py (whole file — confirms WGS84-distance fitting comment)
inject_ai_layer.py (greps for refraction/aether) — only WIN-016 'Annual aberration refractive model' label and the WIN-002 mechanism string

GitHub code search via authenticated curl: total_count = 0 for both '8619 repo:john09289/predictions' and 'aether repo:john09289/predictions' from the search API endpoint, which appears to apply additional indexing filters; direct file fetches via /contents/ DO return the matches, so the absence in search-API hits is an artefact of GitHub code search, not absence in the repo.
code_search.found_in: monitor.py: H0 = 8537.0 (line 211, ECM CONSTANTS LOCKED), LAMBDA_G = 8619.0 (line 212), reused at lines 464 (gravity g(r) decay), 762 (h_D = H0/100). model.html n(r) and H(r) formulas use the same 8537 and 8619 verbatim. find_curve.py: docstring 'Required H(lat) to maintain perfect WGS84 map radius (r = (90-lat)*111.1)' confirms WGS84 fitting target.
code_search.notes: The single 'LOCKED' comment in the ECM CONSTANTS block is the only documentation. There is no derivation comment, no source citation, no measurement reference, no link to model.html for either H0 or LAMBDA_G. This is the strongest possible code-level evidence that these are fitted parameters re-used as physical constants. The gh CLI is not installed in this environment; the curl-based GitHub search API path returned 0 hits even for known-present strings (8619, aether), so the file-content evidence above was obtained via /repos/.../contents/ direct fetches, which always return the actual file. The contents-API evidence is authoritative and reproducible — anyone with read access to john09289/predictions can re-run the same fetches.

deeper_analysis: This fingerprint is intentionally complementary to SEC-4.9 and SEC-2.9 rather than redundant with them. SEC-4.9 shows mathematically that dome n(r) approximates the AE conformal factor; SEC-2.9 shows algebraically that the V13 formula forces n(r) = k·colat/sin(colat); this fingerprint shows in code that the constants going into n(r) are LOCKED, fitted to globe distances, and recycled across other physical predictions — meaning even before you do the conformal-factor algebra, the constants themselves already betray that the refraction formula is curve-fit machinery, not physics. The three fingerprints stack: (a) the constants are globe-fitted (this finding plus SEC-2.1), (b) the formula they go into is the AE conformal factor (SEC-4.9), (c) the formula is algebraically forced by V13 self-consistency (SEC-2.9). Independently each is suggestive; together they are difficult to escape. A dome defender would have to argue that (a) the WGS84 calibration is 'pragmatic' rather than fundamental, (b) the AE-conformal match is coincidence, AND (c) the V13 algebraic identity is meaningless. Each of those arguments is implausible alone; demanding all three at once is a non-starter for a careful reader. The Section 1.5 paragraph that already says 'a perfect n(r) function would be mathematically equivalent to projecting a sphere onto a disc and then undoing the projection' is the right thesis; this finding plus the existing fingerprints supplies the supporting evidence at three different levels of abstraction (constants, geometry, algebra).
newsworthy: The dome's 'aetheric medium' constants are LOCKED Python literals fitted to globe distances and recycled as the gravity, refraction, and dome-height scales — three 'independent confirmations' that are the same WGS84 fit dressed in different vocabulary
tldr: monitor.py declares H0 = 8537 and LAMBDA_G = 8619 as 'ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)' with no derivation, then re-uses them as the scale-length of three unrelated dome predictions (refraction, gravity, sub-terrestrial dome). Section 2.1 already showed both numbers come from a curve-fit against WGS84 city-pair distances. So the dome's 'aetheric medium scale' is not a physical quantity — it is a globe-distance curve-fit residual recycled across domains, which makes the agreement of refraction, gravity, and dome-height predictions one fit instead of three confirmations.
